topic: Prepositions Part 2 — Direction & Movement (To, From, Into, Onto, Through, Across, Along)
keyPointsSummary: 'Into' shows entering, 'Onto' shows moving on top of
'Through' means passing inside something, 'Across' means from one side to another, 'Along' means moving beside something
Common mistake: using "in" instead of "into" for movement

quickQuiz.answer: The cat went into the room.

quickQuiz.hindi: हम जंगल के बीच से गुजरे।
वह सड़क के उस पार गया।
मैं ऑफिस से घर जा रहा हूँ।
quickQuiz.answer: I am going home from the office.
The children climbed onto the bus.

mustRememberRules: 'Into' shows entering, 'Onto' shows moving on top of
'Through' means passing inside something, 'Across' means from one side to another, 'Along' means moving beside something
Common mistake: using "in" instead of "into" for movement
